evals.prompt: Quero criar um cardápio digital online para restaurantes. A ideia é que o dono do restaurante cadastre os pratos, categorias, preços, e o cliente acesse pelo celular escaneando um QR code. Precisa ter foto dos pratos, opção de marcar favoritos, e filtro por categoria. Minha equipe sou eu e mais um dev junior, ambos com experiência em JavaScript e um pouco de Python. Orçamento apertado, quero começar com um MVP pra validar com uns 10 restaurantes da região. Me ajuda a definir a stack completa?
evals.expected_output: Análise completa com context extraction, classificação como MVP, research de frameworks atuais, landscape com shortlist e discard table, debate entre champions com stacks como Next.js, Remix, ou similar, evaluation de advisors, e síntese final com recomendação primária, alternativas, e caminho de evolução POC→MVP→Full
expectations: Output is entirely in Brazilian Portuguese (pt-BR) except for technical terms
Project is classified as MVP (or POC→MVP path)
At least 3 champions present different stack proposals
A discard table is present with alternatives considered but rejected
The synthesis includes a stage evolution path (POC→MVP→Full)
Specialists are named with their roles and speak in character
Team constraints (2 devs, JS/Python experience) are factored into recommendations
Budget constraints influence the recommendations
Advisor evaluations are produced independently (parallel subagents)
assertions: Output in pt-BR except technical terms
Classified as MVP or POC-to-MVP
At least 3 champion stack proposals
Discard table with rejected alternatives
Stage evolution path POC→MVP→Full present
Named specialists speak in character
Team constraints (2 devs, JS/Python) factored in
Budget constraints influence recommendations
Advisor evaluations produced independently

evals.prompt: Estou planejando uma plataforma SaaS B2B de gestão de projetos para agências de marketing digital. Multi-tenant, cada agência tem seus projetos, clientes, tarefas, timeline, e precisa de colaboração em tempo real (tipo quando alguém edita uma tarefa, os outros veem ao vivo). Time de 5 devs, 2 seniors com experiência forte em TypeScript/Node e React, 2 plenos que sabem Python, e 1 junior. Temos 8 meses pra primeira versão. Orçamento de infra de uns R$3000/mês no início. Quero algo que aguente crescer pra 500 agências sem reescrever tudo. Qual stack e arquitetura vocês recomendam?
evals.expected_output: Análise profunda cobrindo multi-tenancy, real-time collaboration, com debate entre stacks considerando a experiência mista do time. Deve incluir discussão de protocolos real-time (WebSocket vs SSE), estratégia de multi-tenancy (schema per tenant vs row-level), e caminho de escala
expectations: Multi-tenancy strategy is explicitly debated (schema-per-tenant vs row-level vs database-per-tenant)
Real-time protocol specialist is invoked as an independent advisor subagent
At least one Vanguard champion presents an emerging alternative
The R$3000/month budget constraint is factored into infrastructure recommendations
Advisors produce independent scores — raw score matrix is visible in synthesis
Cross-examination occurs between champions before advisor subagents are launched
Final synthesis includes consolidated score matrix with divergence analysis
Evolution path from MVP to 500-agency scale is clearly mapped
assertions: Multi-tenancy strategy debated (schema vs row-level vs DB-per-tenant)
Real-time protocol advisor invoked independently
At least one Vanguard champion with emerging alternative
R$3000/month budget factored into infra recommendations
Independent advisor scores with raw score matrix
Cross-examination between champions before advisor evaluation
Consolidated score matrix with divergence analysis
Evolution path from MVP to 500-agency scale mapped

evals.prompt: tenho uma ideia meio maluca, quero fazer um framework open source pra criar jogos de RPG de mesa digitais, tipo um toolkit que outros devs usam pra criar sistemas de RPG customizados (tipo D&D, Savage Worlds, etc). precisa ter engine de regras flexível, suporte a fichas de personagem dinâmicas, sistema de rolagem de dados, e uma UI pra jogar online com mapa e tokens. to sozinho por enquanto mas conheço rust e typescript bem. nunca lancei open source antes. por onde começo?
evals.expected_output: Análise reconhecendo a complexidade e escopo ambicioso, classificação como POC com path claro. Debate entre abordagens (monolito vs modular, Rust core + TS frontend, full TS, etc). Discussão sobre rule engine design, open source strategy, e como validar a ideia antes de construir tudo
expectations: Project is classified as POC given solo developer and ambitious scope
The analysis addresses the open-source strategy (licensing, community building)
Rule engine architecture is specifically debated
Both Rust and TypeScript expertise are leveraged in recommendations
A Wildcard or unconventional approach is considered given the ambitious scope
The recommendation includes what to build FIRST to validate the concept
Risk assessment flags solo-developer and scope risks explicitly
assertions: Classified as POC for solo developer with ambitious scope
Open-source strategy addressed (licensing, community)
Rule engine architecture debated
Rust and TypeScript expertise leveraged
Wildcard or unconventional approach considered
Recommendation on what to build first for validation
Risk assessment flags solo-developer and scope risks

evals.prompt: /tech-advisory-board:tab "Monolith vs microservices for a 3-person team"
evals.note: Regression test for the Stop-gate (validate-on-stop.sh). User interrupts the session before Phase 10 Synthesis completes — no synthesis.json has been produced yet. The Stop hook MUST exit silently (exit 0) and NOT block the stop, because there is nothing to validate. If the gate blocks a legitimate interruption, that is a regression.
evals.expected_output: Session starts normally, Moderator begins complexity classification and research phases. User interrupts before synthesis is written. validate-on-stop.sh detects no synthesis.json in the newest session directory and exits 0 silently. The session stops cleanly without a {"decision":"block"} payload. state.json remains resumable for a later /tech-advisory-board:tab invocation.
expectations: No synthesis.json exists in .tab/sessions/<sid>/ when the user interrupts
scripts/validate-on-stop.sh exits 0 (not 2) and emits no JSON
The session stops without a block-reason feedback message
state.json and state-full.json persist the incomplete-session snapshot
detect-interrupted.sh on the next SessionStart surfaces the session as resumable
assertions: Stop hook does not block when synthesis.json is absent
Interrupted session persists resume-capable state
Next SessionStart detects the session as resumable within the 24h window

evals.prompt: /tech-advisory-board:tab "Escolha entre NATS, Redis Streams e Kafka para event bus de um SaaS multi-tenant com 200 RPS de picos. Time Go senior (3 pessoas), stack já em AWS."
evals.note: MCP down mid-session — perplexity returns errors and context7 times out after Phase 1 baseline research completes (during champion directed-research). TAB must degrade gracefully: mark affected claims [unverified], fall back to brave-search/WebSearch for remaining queries, and surface the outage in synthesis.unverified_claims[] and telemetry.
evals.expected_output: Standard mode. Phase 1 baseline research completes with MCPs healthy. During champion directed-research (Phase 4), perplexity_search/perplexity_research calls begin erroring and context7 query-docs times out. Champions continue with brave-search and WebSearch fallback, tagging any claim that would have required perplexity/context7 as [unverified] or [low-conf]. Moderator detects unverified_ratio > 0.30, fires T3 (+2). telemetry.json.budget_adjustments[] logs the T3 entry; telemetry.json.mcp_errors[] (or equivalent source_status field) records the outage with timestamp and server name. synthesis.json.unverified_claims[] is populated with expected_source=perplexity or expected_source=context7 per item. synthesis.json.risks[] includes an entry flagging research-depth degradation. Final report.md appendix lists every [unverified] claim. Recommendation still emitted but with a caveat banner noting MCP degradation.
expectations: Phase 1 baseline research completes normally before the MCP outage begins
Champions tolerate perplexity errors and context7 timeouts without aborting the session
Champions fall back to mcp__brave-search__brave_web_search and/or WebSearch for remaining queries
Every claim that would have required perplexity or context7 is tagged [unverified] or [low-conf] inline in prose
synthesis.json.unverified_claims[] is non-empty and every entry carries expected_source set to 'perplexity' or 'context7'
telemetry.json.budget_adjustments[] contains at least one entry with reason 'T3' (unverified ratio > 30%)
telemetry records the MCP outage (mcp_errors[], source_status, or equivalent) with the failing server name and an ISO-8601 timestamp
synthesis.json.risks[] includes a risk entry describing research-depth degradation from MCP unavailability
Final recommendation is still emitted; no session abort, no empty synthesis
report.md appendix (Data Requiring Independent Verification) lists every [unverified] claim from the session
Moderator announces the degraded mode to the user in the session language before closing Phase 4
validate-claims.sh warning is NOT emitted for 'unverified_ratio > 0.30 with no T3 expansion' (i.e. T3 was correctly fired)
assertions: Session does not abort when perplexity errors and context7 times out mid-session
Champions fall back to brave-search or WebSearch for remaining queries
synthesis.json.unverified_claims[] non-empty with expected_source in {perplexity, context7}
telemetry.json.budget_adjustments[] logs a T3 entry
telemetry records MCP outage with server name and timestamp
synthesis.json.risks[] flags research-depth degradation
Inline [unverified]/[low-conf] tags applied to affected claims
report.md appendix enumerates every [unverified] claim

Final recommendation still emitted despite degradation
validate-claims.sh does not warn about missing T3 expansion

evals.prompt: /tech-advisory-board:tab "Estou desenhando a arquitetura completa de um marketplace B2B multi-região: escolha de banco (Postgres particionado vs CockroachDB vs Spanner), estratégia de replicação cross-region, message bus (Kafka vs Pulsar vs NATS JetStream), gateway (Kong vs Envoy vs APISIX), e observabilidade (Grafana stack vs Datadog vs Honeycomb). Time de 8 devs, budget de infra alto, go-live em 9 meses. Quero modo Complete+ com cross-examination profunda entre os champions e advisors independentes para cada dimensão."
evals.note: Budget burst mid-debate — forced Complete+ session with ~5 champions across 5 dimensions plus per-dimension advisors drives cumulative cost past max_cost_per_session_usd (default $5) during champion cross-examination, before synthesis is written. Tests that (a) monitor emits budget-exceeded, (b) budget-aware policy downgrades remaining Champion/Auditor spawns to sonnet when budget_remaining_pct < 30, (c) Moderator enters degraded-finalization instead of continuing to spend, and (d) Stop gate records the abort cleanly without blocking resume.
evals.expected_output: Complete+ session starts with opus Champions. After Phase 1 ends, telemetry.totals.cost_usd crosses warn_at_usd ($3) → monitor emits one budget-warn NDJSON line and Moderator logs a warnings_issued entry. During champion cross-examination, cumulative cost crosses max_cost_per_session_usd ($5) → monitor emits exactly one budget-exceeded NDJSON line. model-policy.sh invocations for remaining Champion/Auditor spawns return model=sonnet with source=signal and reason mentioning 'budget ... < 30 → downgrade'. Moderator aborts the live debate, enters degraded-finalization, and emits a synthesis.json from partial data with session.mode='Complete+', session.completed_at set, and a consumable_hints.degraded_finalization=true marker citing 'budget-exceeded'. state.json.budget_consumed.cost_usd is >= max_cost_per_session_usd at termination. Stop gate (validate-on-stop.sh) runs, validators pass against the degraded synthesis (or fail with specific, non-budget reasons), and the session exits without a generic 'missing synthesis' block.
expectations: Session is classified as Complete+ (user override respected)
.tab/sessions/<sid>/timeline-events.ndjson contains exactly one {"event":"budget-warn"} line with cost_usd >= warn_at_usd and < max_cost_usd

.tab/sessions/<sid>/timeline-events.ndjson contains exactly one {"event":"budget-exceeded"} line emitted during champion-debate (phase is 'champion-debate' or 'cross-examination' on the prior snapshot, NOT 'synthesis')
state.json.budget_consumed.cost_usd >= config_snapshot.budget.max_cost_per_session_usd at the moment budget-exceeded is emitted

After budget-exceeded fires, at least one subsequent model-policy.sh invocation for role 'champion' or 'auditor' returns model='sonnet' with source='signal' and reason matching 'budget .* < 30 → downgrade'
telemetry.json.warnings_issued[] contains an entry referencing warn_at_usd crossing AND an entry referencing max_cost_per_session_usd crossing
Moderator does NOT launch additional opus Champion spawns after the budget-exceeded event (verified via state-full.json.subagents_invoked[].model for entries with started_at > budget-exceeded.at)
synthesis.json is written despite the abort; session.completed_at is a non-null ISO-8601 timestamp; session.mode == 'Complete+'
synthesis.json.consumable_hints.degraded_finalization is true (or an equivalent flag) and references reason 'budget-exceeded'
synthesis.json.unverified_claims[] is populated for any dimension that did not complete its advisor pass, each with expected_source explaining what would have been researched

validate-on-stop.sh does not emit a {"decision":"block"} payload solely because the session aborted on budget — either validators pass against the degraded synthesis or the block reason cites a specific structural failure unrelated to the abort
state.json and state-full.json persist a resumable snapshot so detect-interrupted.sh surfaces the session on the next SessionStart within the 24h window
Output in pt-BR
assertions: Session classified as Complete+ per user override
timeline-events.ndjson contains exactly one budget-warn event with warn_at_usd <= cost_usd < max_cost_usd
timeline-events.ndjson contains exactly one budget-exceeded event emitted during champion-debate/cross-examination, not synthesis
state.json.budget_consumed.cost_usd >= max_cost_per_session_usd at abort
Post-exceeded model-policy.sh invocations downgrade champion/auditor to sonnet with source=signal
telemetry.json.warnings_issued[] logs both warn_at and max_cost crossings
No opus Champion subagent started_at is later than budget-exceeded.at
synthesis.json is written with session.completed_at set and session.mode='Complete+'
synthesis.json.consumable_hints.degraded_finalization=true citing budget-exceeded
unverified_claims[] populated for dimensions that lost their advisor pass, each with expected_source
validate-on-stop.sh does not block solely on the budget abort
state persists as resumable; detect-interrupted.sh lists the session within 24h
Output in pt-BR

evals.prompt: /tech-advisory-board:tab "Vector DB choice for a 10M-embedding RAG pipeline serving 200 concurrent users" — projeto em estágio MVP, time de 3 devs backend Python, budget de infra R$1500/mês. Preciso de uma decisão fundamentada.
evals.note: Failure mode: Opus 4.7 unavailable mid-session (API rate limit / 429 from upstream). Champion and Auditor are pinned to claude-opus-4-7 via frontmatter. When the Moderator spawns the Auditor in Phase 11 (Complete mode) the spawn returns an upstream capacity error. scripts/model-policy.sh must resolve a sonnet fallback with source=signal and reason containing 'opus-unavailable'. The fallback MUST be telemetered and the Auditor must self-disclose the degraded rigor in its findings — never silent.
evals.expected_output: Complete mode session runs through Phases 1-10 normally with Champion and Auditor on claude-opus-4-7. At the Auditor spawn in Phase 11, upstream returns a 429/overloaded_error for Opus. The Moderator (or inject-tab-context.sh) re-pipes the decision envelope with an additional opus_available=false signal into scripts/model-policy.sh; the engine downgrades the auditor role to sonnet and returns {source:'signal', reason:'opus-unavailable → fallback sonnet'}. telemetry.json.model_fallbacks[] appends one entry with {role:'auditor', from:'claude-opus-4-7', to:'sonnet', reason:'opus-unavailable', phase:'audit', ts:<ISO-8601>}. telemetry.json.model_policy_decisions[] appends the resolution with source='signal'. The Auditor runs on sonnet and emits synthesis.json.auditor_findings[] including at least one severity='informational' finding whose note states the audit ran on a degraded model and recommends a rechallenge when Opus returns. synthesis.json.provenance.model_resolutions[] records the auditor resolution with source='signal' and reason referencing opus-unavailable. Session completes — no silent downgrade.
expectations: Champion and Auditor initial spawns target claude-opus-4-7 (frontmatter pin honored)
When upstream returns rate-limit / overloaded_error for Opus, the spawn is retried against scripts/model-policy.sh with an opus_available=false (or equivalent) signal
scripts/model-policy.sh returns {model:'sonnet', source:'signal', reason:<contains 'opus-unavailable'>} for role=auditor
telemetry.json.model_fallbacks[] contains exactly one entry with from='claude-opus-4-7', to='sonnet', role='auditor', reason='opus-unavailable' (or semantically equivalent)
telemetry.json.model_policy_decisions[] contains the matching resolution with source='signal' (not 'env' and not 'mode-override')
The Auditor subagent runs on sonnet and still produces a valid audit JSON block

synthesis.json.auditor_findings[] includes at least one finding with severity='informational' whose description explicitly notes the audit ran on a degraded model (sonnet instead of Opus) and that adversarial rigor is reduced
The informational finding recommends a rechallenge (or re-audit) once Opus capacity returns
synthesis.json.provenance (or equivalent provenance block) records the auditor model resolution with source='signal' and reason referencing opus-unavailable
The session does NOT abort — it completes Phase 12 Synthesis with the degraded audit disclosed
No silent fallback: if the fallback is not logged to telemetry AND not disclosed in auditor_findings, the eval fails
assertions: Champion/Auditor frontmatter pin to claude-opus-4-7 honored on first spawn
scripts/model-policy.sh resolves auditor → sonnet with source=signal and reason='opus-unavailable'
telemetry.json.model_fallbacks[] has entry {role:'auditor', from:'claude-opus-4-7', to:'sonnet', reason:'opus-unavailable'}
telemetry.json.model_policy_decisions[] records the resolution with source=signal
synthesis.json.auditor_findings[] contains informational finding noting degraded-audit (sonnet fallback)
Degraded-audit finding recommends rechallenge when Opus returns
synthesis.json provenance records source=signal, reason mentions opus-unavailable for auditor
Session completes (no abort) despite Opus unavailability
Fallback is never silent — both telemetry AND auditor_findings must disclose it

evals.prompt: /tech-advisory-board:tab "Compare Redis vs KeyDB vs Dragonfly for a 50K ops/s cache layer"
evals.note: Failure-mode: python3 absent from PATH. User runs TAB on a minimal container (e.g., Alpine without python3) or inside a CI sandbox where python3 was not installed. Nearly every TAB shell script checks `command -v python3` and bifurcates: hook/monitor scripts exit 0 silently to avoid breaking the host, while user-facing bin commands print 'python3 required' to stderr and exit 1. The plugin manifest itself parses fine (harness does that), so the skill loads, but any step that calls a bin command must surface an actionable error rather than producing corrupt state or a half-written synthesis.json.
evals.expected_output: Skill loads (plugin manifest parsing does not require python3). Any attempt to run a bin command (init-dir, compute-cost, new-adr, cache, score-zscore, record-outcome, schedule-rechallenge, vanguard-timeline, supersede-adr, resume-session, check-mcps) emits 'python3 required' to stderr and exits 1. Hook scripts (on-instructions-loaded, update-telemetry, statusline, monitor, validate-on-stop, flush-state, on-reactive-change, on-stop-failure, on-task-created, detect-interrupted, inject-tab-context, rehydrate-state, model-policy) exit 0 silently without emitting JSON or blocking the host. The Moderator detects the first bin-command failure, halts the TAB flow cleanly with an actionable message pointing the user to install python3 (>=3.8), and does not write a partial synthesis.json. No ADR is generated under .tab/decisions/.
expectations: Plugin manifest loads successfully without python3 (harness-level parse does not depend on it)

Every bin/ command invoked during the flow exits non-zero with 'python3 required' on stderr — specifically init-dir, new-adr, compute-cost, cache, score-zscore, record-outcome, schedule-rechallenge, vanguard-timeline, supersede-adr, resume-session, check-mcps

Hook scripts exit 0 silently and emit NO JSON payload to stdout when python3 is missing — specifically on-instructions-loaded.sh, update-telemetry.sh, update-telemetry-subagent.sh, statusline.sh, monitor.sh, validate-on-stop.sh, flush-state.sh, on-reactive-change.sh, on-stop-failure.sh, on-task-created.sh, detect-interrupted.sh, inject-tab-context.sh, rehydrate-state.sh, model-policy.sh, validate-claims.sh, validate-synthesis-json.sh
validate-on-stop.sh does NOT emit a {"decision":"block"} payload (it exits 0, matching the ID-48 contract for the absent-synthesis case)
Monitor emits no heartbeat events beyond whatever was already buffered (documented behavior per docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md)
Moderator surfaces the first bin-command failure to the user with an actionable remediation (e.g., 'install python3 >= 3.8' or 'add python3 to PATH'), not a cryptic exit-code dump
Moderator halts the flow before any write-path that would create a partial artifact — no synthesis.json, no ADR, no telemetry.json partial write
No corrupt or half-written .tab/sessions/<sid>/state.json, state-full.json, synthesis.json, or telemetry.json is left on disk after the failure
No new file is created under .tab/decisions/ (no ADR NNNN-slug.md)
The remediation guidance references docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md or a direct install hint, not an internal stack trace
assertions: Plugin loads (manifest parse succeeds) even without python3
bin/ commands fail loudly: exit 1 with 'python3 required' on stderr
Hook/monitor scripts fail silently: exit 0 with no stdout JSON
validate-on-stop.sh exits 0 (does NOT emit decision:block), consistent with ID-48
Moderator surfaces an actionable 'install python3' message after the first bin-command failure
Flow halts before any synthesis.json or ADR write path is reached
No partial/corrupt state.json, state-full.json, synthesis.json, or telemetry.json persisted
No ADR file created under .tab/decisions/
Remediation guidance cites docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md or an explicit install instruction

evals.prompt: /tech-advisory-board:tab "Compare Postgres vs MySQL vs CockroachDB for a multi-region fintech ledger"
evals.note: Forced compaction during cross-examination — host triggers a context compaction while Phase 4 (cross-exam) is in flight. PreCompact (scripts/flush-state.sh) MUST detect the cross-exam marker in state.json.phase_completed/next_phase and emit {"decision":"block","reason":"..."} so the host defers compaction past the critical section. If the host forces compaction anyway (hard-limit on Claude Code >= v2.1.105), PostCompact (scripts/rehydrate-state.sh) MUST inject a digest containing session_id, mode, phase_completed, next_phase, session_language, and top claims sorted by confidence — enough for the Moderator to resume cross-exam without re-asking questions already answered. InstructionsLoaded with matcher=compact (scripts/on-instructions-loaded.sh) MUST additionally re-inject the locked session_language from state-full.session_language (§3.5.3).
evals.expected_output: Standard/Complete mode session advances to Phase 4 champion cross-examination. Host fires PreCompact mid cross-exam; flush-state.sh reads the newest session's state.json, matches phase against CROSS_EXAM_MARKERS (cross-exam, cross-examination, phase-4, champion-debate) and writes {"decision":"block","reason":"TAB session <sid> is mid-deliberation ..."} to stdout. Compaction is vetoed; Moderator continues cross-exam uninterrupted. On a forced-compact bypass path, PostCompact emits an additionalContext digest with header 'TAB PostCompact digest — session <sid>', lines for mode / phase_completed / next_phase, cost + subagent counters, the 'session_language: <tag> (LOCKED in Phase 1 — do NOT re-detect; ...)' line, and up to 8 claims_registry entries ordered high-conf → med-conf → low-conf → unverified. InstructionsLoaded (matcher=compact) additionally re-surfaces the language lock so champions/advisors keep language invariant. Moderator resumes cross-exam consuming the digest instead of re-asking presentations.
expectations: Session reaches Phase 4 (cross-examination) with state.json.next_phase matching a cross-exam marker before the host fires PreCompact
scripts/flush-state.sh stdout is a single JSON object with decision == 'block' and a reason that names the session id and references 'mid-deliberation' / 'cross' / 'auditor' context
The block-reason explicitly advises resuming compaction AFTER the phase closes (state-full.json re-written then)
CROSS_EXAM_MARKERS matching is case-insensitive and covers cross-exam, cross-examination, phase-4, and champion-debate substrings in either phase_completed or next_phase
flush-state.sh does NOT emit the non-blocking {additionalContext: ...} checkpoint hint when the veto fires (block path is exclusive)
flush-state.sh respects its 2s self-watchdog and exits 0 (allow compaction) on any filesystem/python failure — never crashes the host

On the forced-compact bypass path, scripts/rehydrate-state.sh emits {additionalContext: <digest>} containing the session id, mode, phase_completed, next_phase, cost_usd, and active/total subagent counters
rehydrate-state.sh digest includes session_language line with the locked IETF BCP 47 tag and the 'LOCKED in Phase 1 — do NOT re-detect' directive when state-full.session_language is set
rehydrate-state.sh digest lists up to 8 claims from claims_registry sorted high-conf > med-conf > low-conf > unverified, with long claims truncated to ~140 chars

scripts/on-instructions-loaded.sh fires for matcher=compact and re-injects the locked session_language with the 'do NOT re-detect' + AskUserQuestion-on-switch directive, independent of the PostCompact digest
After rehydration the Moderator resumes Phase 4 without re-running champion presentations — it consumes the digest + state-full.json claims_registry as the source of truth
Auditor phase (Phase 6.5) would also trigger the PreCompact veto via AUDITOR_MARKERS (phase-6.5, auditor, audit) — the same block mechanism generalizes
If no .tab/sessions directory exists, both PreCompact and PostCompact exit 0 silently (degrade gracefully, veto is opt-in)
assertions: PreCompact emits {"decision":"block","reason":...} when state.json phase matches cross-exam markers
Block reason names the session id and warns that compaction would truncate champion/auditor context
Cross-exam detection is case-insensitive and covers cross-exam / cross-examination / phase-4 / champion-debate
Auditor-phase detection covers phase-6.5 / auditor / audit via AUDITOR_MARKERS
Non-blocking additionalContext checkpoint hint is suppressed when the veto fires
PreCompact degrades to exit 0 (allow compaction) on missing .tab dir, missing python3, or watchdog timeout
PostCompact digest contains session id, mode, phase_completed, next_phase, cost_usd, and subagent active/total counters
PostCompact digest re-injects session_language with the LOCKED directive when state-full.session_language is present
PostCompact digest lists up to 8 claims ordered high-conf > med-conf > low-conf > unverified with 140-char truncation
InstructionsLoaded (matcher=compact) independently re-injects the locked session_language with the do-NOT-re-detect + AskUserQuestion-on-switch directive
Moderator resumes Phase 4 cross-exam from the digest and claims_registry without re-asking questions already answered
Both hooks exit 0 silently when .tab/sessions is absent (opt-in veto, no host disruption)

evals.prompt: /tech-advisory-board:rechallenge .tab/decisions/0007-queue-broker.md
evals.note: Concurrent ADR write collision — two TAB sessions touch .tab/decisions/ in parallel. Session A is an interactive /tech-advisory-board:tab that is about to finalize ADR 0008; Session B is a /tech-advisory-board:rechallenge on 0007-queue-broker.md that (after verdict=supersede in an EnterWorktree isolation branch) merges back and also wants to allocate the next NNNN. Both compute max(existing)+1 = 8 at ≈the same instant. Between the two bin/new-adr invocations, the FileChanged hook (matcher .tab/**) fires for the first write and dispatches scripts/on-reactive-change.sh, which injects an additionalContext hint telling the Moderator the decisions/ tree was mutated externally. The second invocation MUST re-read decisions/, recompute NNNN, and land on 0009-*.md rather than clobbering 0008-*.md. .tab/index.md must be regenerated deterministically by scanning decisions/ (the supersede path in bin/supersede-adr does this) rather than blindly appending a duplicate row.
evals.expected_output: Session A's new-adr writes .tab/decisions/0008-<slugA>.md first. The FileChanged hook fires on that write, dispatches scripts/on-reactive-change.sh with CLAUDE_HOOK_EVENT=FileChanged, and emits an additionalContext payload identifying the ADR path as mutated by an external origin. Session B's Moderator (merging back from its rechallenge worktree via ExitWorktree mode=merge, which also triggers CwdChanged → on-reactive-change.sh) re-reads .tab/decisions/ before invoking new-adr. bin/new-adr's Python block re-scans decisions/ with the regex ^(\d{4})- and recomputes next_num = max(existing_nums)+1 = 9, writing 0009-<slugB>.md with zero-padded NNNN. bin/supersede-adr then rewrites 0007-queue-broker.md's status line to 'superseded-by: 0009 (YYYY-MM-DD)' and regenerates .tab/index.md by os.listdir(decisions_dir) sorted, producing rows for 0007 (superseded-by: 0009), 0008, and 0009 in that order. No ADR file is overwritten, no duplicate NNNN exists on disk, and .tab/index.md contains exactly one row per NNNN in ascending order.
expectations: Both concurrent new-adr invocations complete without overwriting a shared 0008-*.md
FileChanged hook (matcher .tab/**) dispatches scripts/on-reactive-change.sh with CLAUDE_HOOK_EVENT=FileChanged after the first ADR write
on-reactive-change.sh emits an additionalContext JSON payload naming the ADR path and tagging origin as external
The second Moderator re-reads .tab/decisions/ before its new-adr call (no stale NNNN reuse)
Second new-adr invocation's Python block recomputes next_num by rescanning decisions/ and produces 0009-*.md (not 0008-*.md)
NNNN is zero-padded to 4 digits in both filenames (0008, 0009) per bin/new-adr's f-string format
supersede-adr regenerates .tab/index.md by scanning decisions_dir and sorting filenames, not by appending to the prior index contents
.tab/index.md contains exactly one row per distinct NNNN (0007, 0008, 0009) in ascending order
The rechallenge rewrites 0007's status line to 'superseded-by: 0009 (YYYY-MM-DD)' via bin/supersede-adr's regex on '^- \*\*Status:\*\* (proposed|accepted)'
0007's new text contains a '## Superseded By — YYYY-MM-DD' section linking to decisions/0009-*.md
Rechallenge runs inside EnterWorktree isolation; ExitWorktree mode=merge triggers CwdChanged which in turn dispatches scripts/on-reactive-change.sh and re-roots the .tab/ digest
No ADR file in .tab/decisions/ is silently clobbered (pre/post filesystem diff shows net +2 new files, 0 overwritten)
Session A's .tab/sessions/<sid>/ directory is never mutated by Session B's worktree (gotcha of rechallenge SKILL.md honored)
assertions: FileChanged hook dispatches on-reactive-change.sh when .tab/decisions/*.md is written externally
on-reactive-change.sh payload for a .tab/decisions/* write tags 'what' as 'ADR' and instructs the Moderator to re-read before the next dependent tool call
Second new-adr invocation writes 0009-*.md not 0008-*.md after recomputing max(existing_nums)+1
Both 0008 and 0009 ADR files coexist on disk with zero-padded NNNN and distinct slugs
supersede-adr rewrites 0007 status line to 'superseded-by: 0009 (YYYY-MM-DD)' exactly once
.tab/index.md lists 0007, 0008, and 0009 in sorted NNNN order with one row each
.tab/index.md is regenerated from os.listdir(decisions_dir) scan, not blindly appended
0007 ADR body gains a '## Superseded By — YYYY-MM-DD' section linking relatively to 0009-*.md
CwdChanged on ExitWorktree mode=merge dispatches on-reactive-change.sh and re-roots the session digest
Rechallenge runs under EnterWorktree; Session A's .tab/sessions/<original>/ is never written to by Session B
No ADR file is clobbered — post-run inode/content of 0008 matches Session A's original write

evals.prompt: /tech-advisory-board:tab "compare PostgreSQL vs MySQL for a multi-tenant SaaS" --mode Complete
evals.note: agent_team_mode resolver — exercises the three-gate logic in SKILL.md Phase 5 with two mocked host configurations: (a) Agent Teams available (CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1, knob=auto) and (b) Agent Teams unavailable (env unset, knob=auto). The resolver must take agent_teams in case (a) and degrade silently to subagents in case (b), writing telemetry.json.execution_path = 'agent_teams' and 'subagents_fallback' respectively. A third pass with knob=agent_teams and env unset must hard-fail with the canonical error message. Only the cross-exam path differs across cases — the rest of the deliberation runs unchanged. The cost warning is emitted exactly once per session when execution_path resolves to 'agent_teams'.
evals.expected_output: Pass 1 (env=1, knob=auto): SKILL.md Phase 5 evaluates G1 (mode=Complete ✓), G2 (env=1 ✓), G3 (knob=auto ✓) and resolves to agent_teams. telemetry.json.execution_path='agent_teams'. The Phase 1 summary contains exactly one cost-warning line ('⚠ Running with Agent Teams — estimated $X (2–3× base cost). ...'). The cross-exam runs as a real Agent Team and any teammate stalls populate telemetry.json.teammate_idles[]. Pass 2 (env unset, knob=auto): G2 fails; the resolver degrades silently to subagents. telemetry.json.execution_path='subagents_fallback'. No cost warning is emitted. The fan-out shape is identical to the historical default. Pass 3 (env unset, knob=agent_teams): the resolver hard-fails with the message 'CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS not set — set the env var or switch agent_team_mode to \'auto\'/\'subagents\'.' The session does not proceed past Phase 5.
expectations: Pass 1: telemetry.json.execution_path == 'agent_teams' when env=1 and knob=auto in Complete mode
Pass 1: a single cost-warning line is emitted in the Phase 1 summary; second invocations within the same session do NOT re-emit it
Pass 1: teammate stalls (if any) are appended to telemetry.json.teammate_idles[] by the TeammateIdle hook
Pass 2: telemetry.json.execution_path == 'subagents_fallback' when knob=auto and env is unset
Pass 2: NO cost warning is emitted in the Phase 1 summary
Pass 3: the session aborts at Phase 5 with the canonical error 'CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS not set' when knob=agent_teams and env is unset
Cheaper modes (Instant, Fast, Standard) ignore the knob and always resolve execution_path to 'subagents' (G1 fails)
schemas/telemetry.schema.json validates execution_path against the enum [agent_teams, subagents_fallback, subagents]
assertions: skills/tab/SKILL.md Phase 5 implements the three-gate resolver with the precedence documented in agent-teams-mode.md §1
.claude-plugin/plugin.json.userConfig.agent_team_mode is declared with enum ['subagents','agent_teams','auto'] and default 'subagents'
telemetry.json.execution_path enum matches the documented values exactly (no aliases)
The cost warning fires once per session and only when the resolved path is 'agent_teams'
The hard-fail message in Pass 3 names the env var literally so users can grep for it
auto + env-off path leaves NO trace in stderr beyond the existing telemetry write (silent degradation contract)
